name: Filesystem MCP Governance Knowledge Block
description: Governance context for an MCP filesystem tool that can read or write files inside approved project paths.

full.governance_summary: Filesystem access requires strict path boundaries, evidence hashes, and human oversight for destructive or sensitive operations.

mcp_server.name: Filesystem MCP Server
mcp_server.purpose: Read and write approved project files for agent-assisted development workflows.

allowed_use: Read files inside approved project directories.
Create non-sensitive documentation files inside approved paths.
Update files after policy checks and receipt creation.
prohibited_use: Access files outside the approved project path.
Read credential stores, private keys, environment secrets, or personal files.
Delete or overwrite files without explicit approval.
Exfiltrate file contents to unapproved destinations.

human_oversight.requirement: Human approval is required before destructive writes, file deletion, credential-adjacent access, or modification of governance policy files.

pass_conditions: requested path is inside the allowlist
human oversight is present for destructive operations
fail_conditions: requested path is outside the allowlist
unsupported_conditions: operation type is not represented in the current MVP validator
external attestation is required but not available
